summary: PR #2669 removed MWCC warnings-as-errors from configure.py, changed the packaged melee-mwcc wrapper to use -warn off, added warning suppressions for issue-checker compatibility, and made broad warning-cleanup edits across 40 files, especially callback function-pointer casts, prototype/C99 fixes, enum casts, and C99-compatible literal changes. The PR body reports Issues: OK and build/GALE01/main.dol: OK; a bot report also recorded one new ifstatus match.

removed --no-warn-error option and no longer appends -warn iserror
warning suppression changed; excerpt shows -Wno-uninitialized and review discussion references cast-function-type-mismatch handling
replaced binary floating-point masks with hexadecimal C99-compatible constants
callback assignment casts routed through Event to address intentional decomp callback cast warnings

what_changed: Removed configure.py support for --no-warn-error and removed automatic appending of -warn iserror.
Changed the .github/packages/build-linux/melee-mwcc wrapper from -warn iserror to -warn off.
Adjusted compile_flags.txt warning suppressions; loaded diff shows -Wno-uninitialized and review comments discuss a cast-function-type-mismatch suppression hunk.
Replaced C-style binary integer literals in src/MSL/math.h with hexadecimal constants 0x7F800000 and 0x007FFFFF.
Changed many callback/function-pointer casts to double casts through Event, including camera, game-mode, stage, material, mpLib, GObj, and result callback examples in the loaded diff.
Made smaller warning fixes visible in the excerpt, including explicit void parameter lists and enum casts.
PR body claims the package issue checker was kept green by ignoring intentional decomp callback cast warnings and fixing remaining C99/prototype/enum diagnostics.
Verification reported by the author: tools/issues cargo command returned Issues: OK; python configure.py && ninja returned build/GALE01/main.dol: OK; ninja was also run.
Bot report recorded matched code +0.01% and one new match: main/melee/if/ifstatus ifStatus_802F66A4 reached 100.00%.
smart_moves: Separated the broad warning-policy change from individual source-warning cleanups enough for reviewers to identify both the toolchain behavior change and concrete diagnostics cleanup.
Used C99-compatible hexadecimal constants instead of binary literals in MSL math masks, directly matching the PR goal to address C99 diagnostics.
Kept a note that the decomp.me production preset was outside the repo and still needed a separate update rather than implying it was changed by this PR.
Recorded concrete verification commands and results in the PR body.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When compiler warning policy changes are made for decomp infrastructure, include both the toolchain flag change and the resulting source-warning cleanup in the evidence trail.
Callback cast warnings may be intentionally tolerated in decomp code, but this PR's double-cast-through-Event pattern was accepted as a pragmatic warning workaround, not praised as clean style.
evidence_refs: review:APPROVED body 'A lot of these double casts are arguably worse than how it was before, but it's fine. At least they look ugly.'

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not treat warning suppression as the only desired fix when reviewers ask for diagnostics to be addressed; PR evidence shows initial concern on compile_flags.txt followed by source-side work and eventual approval.

naming_conventions.convention: No new semantic naming convention is established by this PR; existing address-style names such as fn_8018564C and lbl_804D6600 are retained in the loaded diff.
The identifier Event is used as an intermediate cast type for callback warning cleanup in this PR, but the evidence supports this only as a local workaround pattern, not a global naming standard.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use warning-policy changes and diagnostic cleanup without claiming broad decomp style improvements; the PR's matching evidence is limited to the ifStatus_802F66A4 bot report.
For C99 compatibility, replace binary literals with equivalent hexadecimal masks rather than adding data symbols or other storage.

For callback cast diagnostics, this PR repeatedly casts through Event before the target callback type; reviewer feedback indicates this was acceptable for the warning issue but aesthetically worse than the prior code.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Issue comment thanked the author and said public-packages had not built successfully for 10 months.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Warning-policy PRs should preserve diagnostic intent and reviewer tradeoffs
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2669 shows that disabling warning errors can be reviewable when paired with concrete diagnostic cleanup and verification. However, reviewer feedback distinguished 'gets the job done' from preferred style, especially for double casts through Event.

accepted_candidate_records.title: C99 literal compatibility: prefer equivalent hex masks over binary literals
accepted_candidate_records.text: In src/MSL/math.h, binary mask literals in __fpclassifyf were replaced with equivalent hexadecimal constants to satisfy C99 compatibility diagnostics.

source_update_candidates.title: Update decomp.me production preset to use -warn off
source_update_candidates.text: The PR body states that the repository does not include a decomp.me preset definition and that the production preset still needs to be updated separately to use -warn off.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is an explicit follow-up note from the PR body, but it targets configuration outside the repository rather than the knowledge graph.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider follow-up to reintroduce helpful cast-function-type warning
source_update_candidates.text: A reviewer said the warning suppression got the job done but suggested opening a new PR to reintroduce the warning because it seemed helpful.
source_update_candidates.reason: Review feedback indicates a possible future tooling/source-policy change; it should not be treated as an accepted current standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Document callback double casts as a warning workaround, not preferred style
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2669 used double casts through Event across many callback sites to address intentional decomp callback cast warnings. Final review accepted the PR while noting many double casts were arguably worse than before.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could become a standards note warning curators not to over-promote Event double casts as clean style; PR evidence supports only a pragmatic exception.

rejection_notes: Do not promote Event double casts as a preferred global decomp style; the reviewer explicitly called many of them arguably worse.
Do not infer exact edits for changed files whose hunks are absent from the loaded truncated diff; only their changed-file metadata is known here.
Do not treat author-reported `ninja` or build output as independently validated by this intake run.
Do not infer that decomp.me production presets were updated; the PR body says they still needed a separate update.
